qa_pairs.question: What is the policy number for this indexed universal life policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Policy Number: HWP-IUL-2024-7290

qa_pairs.question: What is the initial face amount?
qa_pairs.evidence: Initial Face Amount: $500,000

qa_pairs.question: What is the planned annual premium?
qa_pairs.evidence: Planned Annual Premium: $6,000

qa_pairs.question: What is the current cap rate for the S&P 500 Index Account?
qa_pairs.evidence: A) S&P 500 Index Account (Annual Point-to-Point) / Current Cap: 10.5%

qa_pairs.question: What is the floor rate for all index accounts?
qa_pairs.evidence: Floor: 0% / CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline. The worst case is 0% interest for that year.

qa_pairs.question: What is the guaranteed minimum fixed account interest rate?
qa_pairs.evidence: Fixed account will never be less than 2%

qa_pairs.question: What is the fixed account interest rate?
qa_pairs.evidence: FIXED ACCOUNT: Guaranteed interest rate: 3.0% annually

qa_pairs.question: What is the monthly administrative charge?
qa_pairs.evidence: 5.2 Administrative Charge: $12 per month

qa_pairs.question: What is the premium load percentage?
qa_pairs.evidence: 4.4 PREMIUM LOAD: 3.5% of each premium payment is deducted for expenses.

qa_pairs.question: What is the policy loan interest rate?
qa_pairs.evidence: 7.1 POLICY LOANS: Loan interest: 4% annually (fixed loan rate)

qa_pairs.question: What is the surrender charge in Year 1?
qa_pairs.evidence: Year 1: 10%

qa_pairs.question: After what year does the surrender charge reach 0%?
qa_pairs.evidence: Year 12+: 0%

qa_pairs.question: What is the guaranteed minimum cap rate?
qa_pairs.answer: Caps will never be less than 4%
qa_pairs.evidence: 2.5 GUARANTEED RATES: Caps will never be less than 4%

qa_pairs.question: What is the guaranteed minimum participation rate?
qa_pairs.evidence: Participation rates will never be less than 25%

qa_pairs.question: What is the issue date of this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Date of Issue: October 3, 2024

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum premium payment?
qa_pairs.evidence: 4.1 Minimum: $100 per payment

qa_pairs.question: What company issued this policy?
qa_pairs.evidence: Horizon Wealth Protection Insurance

qa_pairs.question: Who is the primary beneficiary?
qa_pairs.evidence: Primary: Matthew Scott Thompson (Spouse) - 100%

qa_pairs.question: Who is the contingent beneficiary?
qa_pairs.evidence: Contingent: Thompson Family Trust - 100%

qa_pairs.question: What is the current allocation across index and fixed accounts?
qa_pairs.answer: S&P 500 Index: 50%, NASDAQ-100 Index: 30%, Fixed Account: 20%
qa_pairs.evidence: 2.3 CURRENT ALLOCATION: S&P 500 Index: 50% / NASDAQ-100 Index: 30% / Fixed Account: 20%

qa_pairs.question: How is the Cost of Insurance charge calculated?
qa_pairs.answer: (Death Benefit minus Account Value) multiplied by the Monthly COI Rate divided by 1,000
qa_pairs.evidence: 5.1 Cost of Insurance (COI): Deducted monthly based on: (Death Benefit - Account Value) × Monthly COI Rate ÷ 1,000

qa_pairs.question: What is the death benefit option currently selected?
qa_pairs.evidence: Death Benefit Option: Option 1 (Level)

qa_pairs.question: What happens to loaned amounts in terms of interest crediting?
qa_pairs.answer: Loaned amounts earn the fixed account rate (3%), and the net cost of the loan is 1% annually (4% loan rate minus 3% credited).
qa_pairs.evidence: 7.1 POLICY LOANS: Loaned amounts earn fixed account rate (3%) / Net cost: 1% annually

qa_pairs.question: What happens if the index declines in a given year?
qa_pairs.answer: The floor protects at 0%, so no interest is credited for that year but no loss is incurred.
qa_pairs.evidence: 3.2 EXAMPLE: If index declined 5%: Floor protects at 0% / Interest credited: 0% (no loss) / 3.3 CRITICAL: You never lose money due to market decline.

qa_pairs.question: If the S&P 500 gains 15% in a year, what interest rate is credited to that account?
qa_pairs.answer: 10.5%, because the gain is capped at the current cap rate.
qa_pairs.evidence: 3.2 EXAMPLE: Assume S&P 500 Index: Gain: 15% / With 100% participation and 10.5% cap / Interest credited: 10.5% (capped)

qa_pairs.question: What is paid if the insured commits suicide within 2 years of issue or reinstatement?
qa_pairs.answer: Only premiums paid minus any withdrawals and charges, without interest.
qa_pairs.evidence: 11.1 SUICIDE: If insured commits suicide within 2 years of issue or reinstatement, we pay only premiums paid minus any withdrawals and charges, without interest.

qa_pairs.question: When will the insurer notify the policy owner before a lapse occurs?
qa_pairs.evidence: 10.1 If account value is insufficient to pay monthly deductions, we will notify you at least 30 days before lapse.

qa_pairs.question: How many free partial withdrawals are allowed per year before fees apply?
qa_pairs.answer: The first 10 withdrawals are free; a $50 fee applies thereafter.
qa_pairs.evidence: 7.2 PARTIAL WITHDRAWALS: First 10 withdrawals free; $50 fee thereafter

qa_pairs.question: When may allocation changes be made without a fee?
qa_pairs.answer: On any policy anniversary; the first 4 reallocations per year are free, with a $25 fee thereafter.
qa_pairs.evidence: 2.4 You may change allocations on any policy anniversary at no charge. First 4 reallocations per year are free; $25 fee thereafter.

qa_pairs.question: After what year can the policy owner make free partial withdrawals of up to 10% of account value without surrender charge?
qa_pairs.evidence: 8.4 FREE PARTIAL WITHDRAWAL: You may withdraw up to 10% of account value each year without surrender charge after Year 1.

qa_pairs.question: After how many years is a policy loan available?
qa_pairs.evidence: 7.1 POLICY LOANS: Available after 1 year

qa_pairs.question: Under Option 1, if the account value grows over time, what happens to the cost of insurance charge?
qa_pairs.answer: The COI charge decreases, because it is calculated as (Death Benefit minus Account Value) times the COI rate; as the account value grows, the net amount at risk decreases, reducing the COI.
qa_pairs.evidence: 6.1 OPTION 1 - Level Death Benefit (CURRENT): Death Benefit = $500,000 (specified amount) / 5.1 Cost of Insurance (COI): Deducted monthly based on: (Death Benefit - Account Value) × Monthly COI Rate ÷ 1,000

qa_pairs.question: If the S&P 500 index gains 8% in a year, what interest is credited to the S&P 500 Index Account, and is the cap invoked?
qa_pairs.answer: 8% interest is credited. The cap of 10.5% is not invoked because the gain is below the cap rate.
qa_pairs.evidence: A) S&P 500 Index Account (Annual Point-to-Point) / Current Cap: 10.5% | Participation Rate: 100% / Floor: 0% / 3.1 Interest Credited = Index % Change × Participation Rate (subject to Cap and Floor)

qa_pairs.question: Is this policy considered a securities product, and does index participation mean the policy owner owns stocks?
qa_pairs.answer: No. This is not a securities product. Index participation does not involve direct ownership of stocks or index funds.
qa_pairs.evidence: 12.1 This is NOT a securities product. Index participation does not involve direct ownership of stocks or index funds.

qa_pairs.question: If the No Lapse Guarantee Rider is not elected and the account value drops to zero, will coverage continue?
qa_pairs.answer: No. Without the No Lapse Guarantee Rider, the policy would lapse if account value is insufficient to cover monthly deductions after the 61-day grace period.
qa_pairs.evidence: ARTICLE 9 - NO LAPSE GUARANTEE RIDER (OPTIONAL - NOT SELECTED): Not currently elected on this policy. / 10.2 Grace period: 61 days to pay sufficient premium to bring policy back in force.

qa_pairs.question: Do index credits include dividends from index component stocks?
qa_pairs.answer: No. Dividends from index components are not included in index performance calculations.
qa_pairs.evidence: 12.2 Dividends from index components are NOT included in index performance calculations.

qa_pairs.evidence: N/A
